reasoning: Query asks for little-known remote hot springs (秘湯) in Akita. Response returns 日本秘湯を守る会 member ryokan in Akita — perfectly on-target. Records include street addresses and designation authority. Minor gaps: no coordinates, no website/phone for most, and no transport access info. Expression quality slightly reduced by truncation mid-list.
missing_data: Coordinates, websites, and how to reach each onsen (car-only roads, seasonal closures). More ryokan entries cut off at truncation boundary.
improvement_hint: Add coordinates and access notes (car required, road closures in winter) to hotel records. Surface seasonal closure metadata from the hitou.or.jp source pages.

reasoning: User explicitly asked for islands in Kagoshima that are NOT Yakushima or Amami. Response leads with Sakurajima (not remote or rarely visited) and prominently features Yakushima with its Jōmon Sugi tree — the exact place the user excluded. Constraint filtering completely failed. Truly remote islands (Tokunoshima, Yoronjima, Kuchinoerabujima, Suwanose-jima) do not appear.
missing_data: Tokunoshima, Yoronjima, Kuchinoerabujima, Takara-jima, Suwanose-jima entries. Any spot tagged island that excludes the explicitly named places.
wrong_data: Yakushima and Jōmon Sugi should have been filtered out per the explicit user constraint.
improvement_hint: Implement exclusion handling in get_spots so the agent can pass `exclude_names` or `exclude_ids`. At minimum, rank remote small-island entities (kinds:island, low prominence) higher than Yakushima/Sakurajima when those are explicitly mentioned in the query.

reasoning: Query asks specifically about pottery (Töpfer) traditions using nearly-lost techniques. get_traditional_arts with no keyword returns all 183 ICH items sorted by default order — top results are Bunraku puppet theater and Kabuki, not pottery. The tool entirely misses the specific craft constraint. No ceramics or pottery entries appear in the visible portion.
missing_data: Kyo-yaki, Bizen-yaki, Tokoname-yaki, Karatsu-yaki, Satsuma-yaki or any pottery/ceramics with intangible cultural designation. Keyword filtering on 陶芸/焼物 was not applied.
improvement_hint: When get_traditional_arts is called without a keyword but the user query contains a specific craft term, the server should auto-extract the keyword and apply it as a category filter. Support keyword=陶芸/ceramics routing in the tool schema.

reasoning: Query asks for abandoned railway sites (廃線跡) specifically in Kyushu, excluding well-known tourist sites. Response retrieves 84 matches but leading results include 豊後森機関庫 (now a park/museum in Oita — already tourist-developed), a Hokuriku tunnel heritage site in Fukui (not Kyushu), and a DMO plan chunk from Shiga (not Kyushu). Prefecture constraint and 'not a tourist site' intent both ignored.
missing_data: Kyushu-specific廃線 records: former Chikuho coal-line relics, Miyazaki Ringyo Railroad ruins, Kumamoto Electric Railway abandoned sections. Prefecture filtering not applied.
wrong_data: 旧北陸線トンネル群 (Fukui) is outside Kyushu and shouldn't rank here.
improvement_hint: Add prefecture_code filter support to search_area, or enable multi-field scoring that penalises off-prefecture results. Return a scope warning when fewer than 3 results match the prefecture constraint.

reasoning: Query (in Chinese) asks about deep Ainu cultural experience in Hokkaido beyond tourist spots. search_hybrid returns high-quality results: 千歳アイヌ文化伝承保存会 (community preservation activities with event calendar), 二風谷コタン (Biratori — traditional crafts, museums, workshop), 北の工房つとむ (working artisan), and a Wikidata cultural landscape entry. Covers multiple Hokkaido municipalities. Minor gap: response is in Japanese not Chinese as requested.
missing_data: ウポポイ (Upopoy National Ainu Museum, Shiraoi) is absent — the flagship national facility opened 2020. Chinese-language descriptions also missing.
improvement_hint: Ensure ウポポイ/白老 is indexed with Ainu-culture kind tags. Improve Chinese-language field coverage in municipal scrape for Ainu-related records.

reasoning: User asks specifically about lesser-known Nakasendo post towns beyond Tsumago. get_japan_heritage for Nagano returns Kamikochi, Matsumoto Castle, Zenkoji — none of which are Nakasendo post towns. The response entirely misses the post-town/宿場 theme. Magome, Narai, Nojiri, Shiojiri-juku, etc. do not appear.
missing_data: Narai-juku, Nojiriko-juku, Shiojiri-juku, Yabuhara-juku, Moriyamajuku — Nakasendo shukuba-machi. Japan Heritage story 'Edo-Tokyo no Nakasen-do' is absent.
improvement_hint: Add 宿場町/post-town kind tags to Nakasendo stations in Wikidata layer. get_japan_heritage should rank stories matching the query theme (中山道 宿場) before generic prefectural attractions.

reasoning: User asks for little-visited hamlets in Oku-Aizu, Fukushima. get_spots(Fukushima) returns castle-dominated must_see tier results (Aizuwakamatsu Castle, Komine Castle, Nihonmatsu Castle) and Oze highland — none specific to Oku-Aizu. The query specifies 'almost no tourists' and 'hamlet', but tool returns high-prominence attractions across all of Fukushima.
missing_data: Oku-Aizu villages: Kaneyama-machi, Mishima-machi, Showa-mura, Tadami-machi (only-wood bridge area). Only-wood bridge (only bridge) and thatched farmhouses of Oku-Aizu are entirely absent.
improvement_hint: Add municipality_code filtering to get_spots so the agent can scope Oku-Aizu specifically. Tag low-prominence rural settlements (集落) as a kind so they surface when user intent is 'hidden hamlet'.

reasoning: Query asks about traditional craft villages (làng nghề) in Tottori. get_local_specialty returns MAFF GI items which are all food products: rakkyo, broccoli, persimmon, watermelon. No craft or artisan entries appear. The user's intent is craft workshops/traditions, not GI food products. Translation to Vietnamese is good but content is completely wrong category.
missing_data: Tottori tamba-yaki pottery, Inaba kasuri weaving, traditional woodcraft from San'in region. METI-designated traditional crafts for Tottori are absent.
improvement_hint: When query intent is clearly crafts/manufacturing rather than food, prefer METI densan items over MAFF GI food. Add a craft/non-food category filter to get_local_specialty or create a clear category-routing hint in the tool description.

reasoning: Query specifically asks 'how do I get there without a car?' — a transport constraint the response entirely ignores. The data about Iya Valley and Kazurabashi is rich and accurate (correct details about dimensions, 3-year re-weaving cycle, Heike legend), but the response contains no transit/bus access information. Additionally, name field is raw HTML img tag which is unusable. Korean text contamination in description fields.
missing_data: Public transit access from Awa-Ikeda station (bus to Kazurabashi). Seasonal bus service details. Car-free route options — this is the explicit constraint.
improvement_hint: Clean HTML img tags from name fields at extraction time. Strip non-target-language text contamination (Korean fragments) from description fields. Add transit_access field populated from DMO transport pages.

reasoning: User asks about obscure religious festivals near Mt. Tsurugi (剣山) in Tokushima. get_festivals returns only 2 Tokushima items — both are summer fireworks shows (花火大会), not religious/shrine ceremonies. National heritage section shows UNESCO ICH items from Akita, Iwate, and Saitama — not Tokushima. The sparse results fail to surface Mt. Tsurugi's Yamabushi rituals or obscure shrine ceremonies.
missing_data: 剣山本宮宝蔵石神社 ceremonies, Yamabushi/Shugendo rituals on Mt. Tsurugi, 東祖谷神代踊 (national intangible cultural property). Festival coverage for Tokushima is sparse.
improvement_hint: Expand festival scraping to include Tokushima municipal sites covering 剣山-area shrine events. Tag Shugendo/yamabushi events as a kind so they surface for mountain-shrine queries.

reasoning: Query (Arabic) asks about isolated mountain villages with traditional lifestyle still inhabited. search_hybrid returns a Jomon-era museum experience in Nagano, a Tobe-yaki ceramics hall in Ehime, and 五家荘 (Kumamoto, relevant — 5 Heike-descendant hamlets). The first two results are off-target. Only one result (五家荘) partially addresses the intent. Gokasho and Ochiai-cluster (Miyoshi-shi) are present but buried.
missing_data: 白川郷/五箇山 (inhabited gassho-zukuri villages), 東祖谷落合集落 (national important preservation district), 椎葉村 (Miyazaki, Heike descendants with traditional farming). More inhabited remote hamlet examples.
improvement_hint: Improve 秘境集落 semantic embedding to prioritize inhabited traditional villages over museum/exhibit experiences. Add kind tag 重要伝統的建造物群保存地区 for preservation districts so they surface for 'traditional lifestyle village' intent.

reasoning: User asks about Noto Peninsula's ancient salt-making tradition and whether remote workshops still operate. get_local_specialty(Ishikawa) returns food GI items: Hakusan sake, Kaga yam, Noto Shika dried persimmon, and ishiri/ishiru fish sauce. No salt-making (揚げ浜式製塩) entry appears despite Noto's famous traditional salt panning being METI-designated. User's specific interest in salt craft is not matched.
missing_data: 能登の揚げ浜式製塩 (METI traditional craft designation), Noto salt workshop locations (珠洲市の製塩業者), post-earthquake operational status notes.
improvement_hint: Ensure 揚げ浜式製塩 is indexed as a METI densan entry for Ishikawa/Noto. Add craft-type filtering to get_local_specialty so food and non-food items can be separated when intent is clearly non-food.

reasoning: User asks specifically about wheelchair barrier-free routes at Himeji Castle. search_hybrid returns Himeji City tourism pages but the top hit is about an AR/VR app — not barrier-free routes. No specific wheelchair access info for Himeji Castle appears. A Nanyō City (Yamagata) バリナビ video shows up irrelevantly. The result titles are all 'くらし・手続きトップ' (municipal portal headers) rather than specific content pages.
missing_data: Himeji Castle official wheelchair access information, which areas are navigable vs. inaccessible (keep don't allow wheelchairs in the main tower), accessible parking/restroom details.
improvement_hint: Add structured accessibility fields (wheelchair_access, accessible_routes) to spot records sourced from official castle/attraction pages. Remove generic city portal pages with nav-chrome titles from search index.

reasoning: User asks for Kyoto temples that are wheelchair accessible with flat stone paths and accessible restrooms. get_spots(Kyoto) returns must_see landmarks (Nijo Castle: wheelchair=limited, Toji: wheelchair=limited) but does not filter for wheelchair=yes and shows many irrelevant items (Nagaoka-kyo archaeological site, generic must_see tier). The explicit accessibility constraint (accessible restrooms, flat paths) is not honored.
missing_data: Temples with wheelchair=yes specifically: 東寺 is marked limited only. Accessibility-confirmed temples like 建仁寺, 高台寺, 渉成園 with accessible features. Restroom accessibility fields absent.
improvement_hint: Add a `wheelchair` filter parameter to get_spots/get_hotels. Return accessible_restroom and step_free_path fields when available from OSM data. Pre-filter results by wheelchair=yes|limited when accessibility is the explicit constraint.

reasoning: User asks about stroller-friendly Kyoto temples with ramps and elevators. search_hybrid returns results from Gifu Science Museum, a Mie resort, a Hokkaido restaurant (mentioning strollers), and 厭離庵 (a Kyoto temple but with no accessibility data). No Kyoto-specific stroller accessibility information appears. Geographic constraint (Kyoto) and content constraint (stroller access) both failed entirely.
missing_data: Kyoto temple stroller access info: 平等院 (ramp available), 金閣寺 grounds accessibility, 嵐山 Tenryuji garden paths. Results from Gifu and Hokkaido are clearly off-target.
improvement_hint: Add prefecture_code filtering to search_hybrid queries. When ベビーカー appears with a location, restrict vector and BM25 retrieval to that prefecture's documents before RRF fusion.

reasoning: User asks for flat, easy-walking courses in Nara for elderly parents in their 70s. get_spots(Nara) returns must_see tier sorted by prominence: Yoshino-yama (steep mountain, very unsuitable), Takamatsuzuka tomb (outdoor site), Kofukuji Temple, Koriyama Castle. Mountain spots listed first directly contradict the mobility constraint. No topography or terrain flatness data available.
missing_data: Flat routes in Nara Park (鹿苑, Todaiji forecourt) suitable for elderly. 奈良公園バリアフリーマップ data. Wheelchair/elderly suitability field for spots.
improvement_hint: Add terrain_difficulty or step_count field to spot records. Enable filtering by suitable_for=elderly or mobility_limited. Mount Yoshino should be flagged as high-difficulty terrain to avoid appearing for elderly/mobility queries.

reasoning: User (manual wheelchair user) asks for Hakone ryokan with no-step rooms and accessible outdoor baths (rotenburo). get_hotels(Kanagawa) returns: Aoki House (hostel, no accessibility data), Guest house Kamejikan, Hilton Odawara (resort near Odawara, not Hakone), UR apartment complex. No ryokan appears in the visible results, and no accessibility/wheelchair fields are present in any record.
missing_data: Hakone-area ryokan with accessible rooms. No wheelchair_access or barrier_free_bath field in hotel records. Hilton is outside Hakone proper. Hakone ryokan cluster absent.
improvement_hint: Add Hakone municipality filter to ensure results concentrate in the correct area. Add wheelchair_accessible_room and accessible_bath boolean fields to hotel records sourced from OSM/official ryokan pages.

reasoning: User asks for museums with tactile exhibits and audio guides for visually impaired visitors. Top hit (城川視覚体験館) is a visual illusion/trick-of-the-eye experience — the opposite of what a visually impaired person needs. Second result is a barrier-free film screening event notice, not a museum. Third result is a dementia lecture. Essentially no useful results for the specific accessibility intent.
missing_data: Museums with certified tactile exhibits: 国立民族学博物館 (Osaka, touch exhibits), 東京国立博物館 audio guide info, 広島平和記念資料館 accessible exhibits. These major institutions are absent.
wrong_data: 城川視覚体験館 (visual illusion museum) is counterproductive for visually impaired visitors.
improvement_hint: Index major national museums with their accessibility program details. Add audio_guide and tactile_exhibit boolean fields to spot records. Distinguish 視覚体験 (visual trick rooms) from 視覚障害者対応 (accessible for visually impaired).

reasoning: User (French) asks about wheelchair access on Miyajima island to see the torii. search_area returns relevant barrier-free resources including Ise Kanko (with wheelchair map and wheelchair assistance service, mentioning sign language guide), Yokohama accessibility portal, and Shimane barrier-free tourism content. However, none are specifically about Miyajima (Hiroshima). No response is in French. Top result mentions Ise not Miyajima.
missing_data: Miyajima-specific (Hiroshima) wheelchair access information. The torii access path, Omotesando approach surface quality, ferry wheelchair boarding. French language descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add Miyajima/Itsukushima-jinja official barrier-free page to the DMO scrape list. Ensure geographical matching prioritizes Hiroshima results for 宮島 queries.

reasoning: User asks about sign-language or hearing-loop guided tours in Japan. Results include: Iwate barrier-free tourism info (general, not specific to sign language tours), Ise Kanko barrier-free page (explicitly mentions sign language volunteer guides for Ise Jingu — highly relevant), Ishikari city accessibility map, Kagoshima wheelchair universal tour model. The Ise result with 手話サークルによるボランティアガイド is a genuine hit. Coverage is thin but one strong match exists.
missing_data: Other specific sign-language tour programs: Tokyo Accessible Tourism Center, 京都ユニバーサルツーリズム. Hearing loop (ヒアリングループ) facilities at museums or shrines. Only one clear match found.
improvement_hint: Tag DMO pages and spot records that explicitly mention 手話 or ヒアリングループ with an accessibility_features field. Build a richer hearing-accessibility corpus from prefectural tourism boards.

reasoning: German query about wheelchair access to Nikko Toshogu. search_hybrid surfaces: Iwate barrier-free general page, 日光東照宮宝物館 (Wikidata, treasury museum — relevant!), Tochigi DMO plan listing Toshogu as a star attraction, and a Shizuoka DMO plan mentioning Toshogu. No specific barrier-free route or step information for Toshogu itself appears. Results are in Japanese, not German. Toshogu's steep stone steps and hilly terrain are practically important but unaddressed.
missing_data: Toshogu specific accessibility details: steps count, which areas are accessible, wheelchair loan availability at Nikko. German-language content absent.
improvement_hint: Scrape Nikko municipal tourism accessibility pages. Add step_count and wheelchair_loan fields to attraction records. Improve language routing so German queries get German-language responses.

reasoning: User with electric wheelchair asks about barrier-free routes in hilly Nagasaki city. get_spots(Nagasaki, municipality=長崎) returns: Siebold Memorial Museum, Kaneda Castle (on Tsushima island — not Nagasaki city), Shimabara Castle (Shimabara city — not Nagasaki city), Nagasaki City entry. The municipality filter did not narrow to 長崎市 specifically. No barrier-free route info. Tsushima and Shimabara results are geographically wrong.
missing_data: Nagasaki city barrier-free routes: グラバー園 (accessible tram), 大浦天主堂 accessibility, 出島 slope info. Ropeway as an alternative to steep slopes. Mobility scooter rental in Nagasaki.
wrong_data: Kaneda Castle (Tsushima) and Shimabara Castle are not in 長崎市 and should not appear for that municipality filter.
improvement_hint: Fix municipality matching so 長崎 (city) filters only records with municipality_code matching Nagasaki City (422011). Expose wheelchair and step_free fields from municipal scrape accessibility pages.

reasoning: User asks about stroller-friendliness of Asakusa. Top hit (浅草ガイド from gotokyo.org) gives a good general overview of Asakusa and mentions the flat Nakamise approach — relevant contextually but no explicit stroller info. 浅草文化観光センター result mentions facilities. Otaru stroller-rental data is off-topic. Overall result set partially addresses the question with some Asakusa-specific content. No specific pavement/step data for temple precinct.
missing_data: Nakamise-dori explicit step-free status, Senso-ji main hall approach surface, stroller-accessible entrances. Specific pavement descriptions absent.
improvement_hint: Add surface_type (paved/gravel/stone) and step_count fields to spot records. Enrich Asakusa municipal scrape with stroller/accessibility-specific pages from Taito-ku tourism.

reasoning: Thai query asks about accessible wheelchair viewpoints of Mt. Fuji. Top hit (Ehime Habu-yama hiking trail blog) has nothing to do with Mt. Fuji or wheelchair access. Second hit is a Fukui mountain called 'Wakasa Fuji'. Third hit is a Fujimi-cho highland resort mentioning Fuji views. No actual wheelchair-accessible Fuji viewpoint (Kawaguchiko accessible lakeside promenade, Miho-no-Matsubara, Fujisan World Heritage Center in Shizuoka) appears.
missing_data: Kawaguchiko lakeside promenade (wheelchair accessible), Fujisan World Heritage Center Shizuoka (fully accessible), Fujiten accessible viewing areas. Thai-language descriptions absent.
wrong_data: Top result (Habu-yama, Ehime) is completely off-topic for this query.
improvement_hint: Improve 富士山 + バリアフリー semantic embedding. Add kind tags for accessible_viewpoint. The Fujisan World Heritage Center in Fujinomiya should be indexed with wheelchair=yes and Fuji-view tags.

reasoning: User asks about shrines and tourist facilities with Braille, tactile maps, and visually-impaired guidance. Top hit is a Sanriku Tourism website page about language translation — completely off-topic for tactile maps. Second hit is a Fukuoka city library guide mentioning 点字図書館 (Braille library). Third hit is another Sanriku DMO plan. None of the results are about shrines or tourist sites with Braille/tactile infrastructure. Retrieval essentially failed.
missing_data: Shrines/temples with tactile maps: 伊勢神宮 accessible guide, 四天王寺 touch exhibits. National facilities with Braille: 国立博物館, 東大寺 accessible programs. Specific tactile map installations at Japanese heritage sites.
improvement_hint: Index accessible-design features of major shrines/temples when scraping official sites. Add braille_available and tactile_map_available boolean fields to spot/facility records. Build a dedicated accessibility features taxonomy beyond wheelchair.
